title: Aldehydes and ketones
summary: 9701 AS carbonyl chemistry — nucleophilic addition, distinguishing tests, and reduction with PhET molecule builder.
learningObjectives: Describe the polar nature of the carbonyl group.
Explain nucleophilic addition mechanisms to C=O.
Distinguish aldehydes from ketones using Tollens' and Fehling's reagents.
Describe reduction of carbonyls to alcohols using NaBH₄ or LiAlH₄.

simpleExplanation.title: The carbonyl group
simpleExplanation.summary: The C=O bond is polar with δ+ on carbon — nucleophiles attack here. Aldehydes are easier to oxidise than ketones.
steps: Nucleophile attacks C=O → tetrahedral intermediate → protonation gives alcohol.
Aldehydes → carboxylic acids with oxidising agents; ketones resist.
Tollens' reagent (Ag⁺): aldehydes → silver mirror; ketones no reaction.
NaBH₄ reduces aldehydes to primary alcohols; ketones to secondary alcohols.

sections.content: **Carbonyl chemistry** (17.1) is high mark-yield on Paper 2 — mechanisms with **CN⁻**, **HCN**, and **2,4-DNPH** identification tests appear frequently.
